response:
In C# it is possible to estimate the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but it is not as straightforward as with ping  
UDP does not guarantee delivery or timing information  
To measure the time you can use the DateTime class  
Send the packet and record the start time  
Then wait for the response and record the end time  
Calculate the difference between the two times  
This will give you an approximate round trip time  
However this method is not accurate for one direction  
You can use the Stopwatch class for more precise timing  
Make sure to handle exceptions and timeouts properly  
This approach is not reliable for one way communication  
It is recommended to use TCP for reliable communication  
UDP is suitable for one way communication only  
You can use the SendTo method to send the packet  
And the ReceiveFrom method to receive the response  
But the response may not arrive at all  
Therefore the timing is not guaranteed  
This is why ping is used for round trip time measurement  
In conclusion measuring one way time with UDP is not reliable  
But you can estimate it using DateTime or Stopwatch  
Ensure you handle all possible errors and exceptions
